---
title: "jstats with your own data"
subtitle: "Loading, saving, and round-tripping your own files — and handling the missing values that come with them."
---

::: {.callout-warning title="Skeleton — to be drafted"}
This page has its structure in place; the prose is still to be written. The plan
below is ordered from simplest to most involved.
:::

This page is about getting *your* data in and out of jstats — and dealing with the
real-world quirks that come with it, especially missing values. The examples lean
on `community` as a clean default, with `clinic` brought in where dirty-data beats
need it; you can also follow along with your own file loaded alongside, so anything
that can't be shown on your data can be shown on `community`.

## Planned sections

- **Revisit the Project `.Rprofile`:** add `joptions(data.dir = "Data")`; mention
  other optional lines such as `joutput(digits = 3)`.
- **Simplest load:** `jload("community")` brings in the shipped dataset; the "get a
  clean copy back" move with `overwrite = TRUE`.
- **Save and reload your own work:** `jsave` and `jload` with a bare filename; the
  auto-created data folder.
- **Importing foreign formats:** reading your own SPSS (`.sav`), Stata (`.dta`),
  and Excel (`.xlsx`) files. *(The package-shipped copies reached with
  `system.file()` may be demonstrated here or saved for the workflow page — settle
  at drafting.)*
- **The payoff:** one `jload` versus a different package and syntax per format.
- **Missing values:** user-defined missing values and plain `NA`s; how SPSS, Stata,
  and Excel files differ; per-platform sections (read the one that's yours).
- **The SPSS-to-Stata convention switch:** why, and when each is preferable.
- **The data viewer is read-only:** you can't edit cells the way you would in
  commercial software's data view; the spreadsheet-style edit-and-reload path
  belongs here, next to the working-copy-in-memory idea. *(Deferred here from the
  Orientation page by design.)*
- *(Optional)* a second regression on a loaded copy, to close the loop.
